dropImage: Drop an image here or click to select
supportedFormats: Supports PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, BMP, SVG
firstFrameOnly: Animated image — only the first frame is used
largeImage: Large image ({w}×{h}) — processing may be slow
formatNotSupported: This image format is not supported. Please use PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, BMP, or SVG.

descriptions.title: About Image Resizer
descriptions.aeoDefinition: Image Resizer is a free online tool for resizing images by percentage or custom dimensions. Supports PNG, JPG, WebP input. All processing runs locally in your browser.
descriptions.whatIs: Resize images by percentage or custom dimensions directly in your browser. Maintain aspect ratio or set exact pixel dimensions. No data is uploaded to any server — all processing uses the HTML5 Canvas API.
descriptions.step1Text: Drag and drop an image onto the drop zone, or click to browse. Supports PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, BMP, and SVG input.
descriptions.step2Text: Choose resize by percentage (1–400%) or enter exact width and height in pixels. Toggle aspect ratio lock as needed.
descriptions.step3Text: Preview the resized image and download it. The output format matches the input format.
descriptions.p1: Resize images by percentage or custom dimensions directly in your browser. Compress images with [Image Compressor](/image-compress), or convert formats with [Image Converter](/image-convert).
descriptions.p2: Supports PNG, JPG, and WebP output. Images in GIF, BMP, SVG, or AVIF format are automatically converted to PNG.
descriptions.faq1Q: What image formats can I resize?
descriptions.faq1A: You can resize PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, BMP, SVG, and AVIF images. The output format matches the input format. Unsupported output formats (GIF, BMP, SVG, AVIF) are saved as PNG.
descriptions.faq2Q: Are my images uploaded to a server?
descriptions.faq2A: No. All image processing happens in your browser using the Canvas API. Your images never leave your device.
descriptions.faq3Q: How do I resize without distorting the image?
descriptions.faq3A: Enable the "Keep aspect ratio" option when using custom dimensions. The image will be resized to fit within the specified dimensions while maintaining its original proportions.
